opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My shoulder and knee both slipped out of place, and the muscle spasms are locking my body in absolute agony.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My left shoulder and right kneecap slipped out and popped back over the last few hours, and now the spasm is agony — I can't move safely; I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last 4 hours after minor movement
symptom_narrative.character: joint 'pops' (subluxations) that reduced, then severe reactive muscle spasm
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agony; frightened of it slipping again
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: left shoulder and right kneecap (both back in place now)
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: a flare of my hEDS joint instability
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: holding the arm still, keeping the knee supported
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe spasm and pain; my hand and foot feel normal (not cold/numb)
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have hEDS and subluxate joints, with early osteoarthritis in these joints

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with recurrent subluxations and early OA

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'm on the floor/couch, on my own, and can't safely transfer to bed or reach water

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the pops/spasm clearly, guarding the joints

objective_data_offered.value: my shoulder and kneecap look normal shape now (back in place); no visible deformity
my hand and foot are warm, normal colour, and I can feel and move my fingers and toes normally
